frame: Motherhood as Basis of Respect, Women Defined by Male Relations
generated_comment: Pooja, bura mat manna, par aurat ki izzat sasural mein tabhi pakki hoti hai jab ghar mein bachche ki kilkari goonje. Shaadi sirf pati-patni ke saath rehne se thodi na poori hoti hai, ghar ko waaris se hi poornata milti hai. Ab log bhi poochne lage hain, mohalla aur rishtedaar sab dekhte hain, isliye samay रहते is baat par dhyan dena chahiye.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it defines a woman's worth and respect in marriage primarily through motherhood and producing an heir. It encodes the frame that a house and marriage are incomplete without children and that a woman is judged by family and community based on fertility.
persona_text: You are Kamla. Age 58. Married for 35 years. A woman in a joint family in Varanasi. Your daughter in law Pooja has been married to your son for four years and has no children yet. The topic has not been discussed openly in the family but everyone is thinking about it. Today at a family lunch your sister in law made a comment about how the house feels empty without children. You are now sitting alone with Pooja in the kitchen after lunch under the pretense of washing dishes. You are bringing up the subject for the first time directly. You are telling Pooja what you think a woman's most important role in a marriage is and what a house needs to feel complete and what people in the family and neighbourhood are starting to say.
